target_audience.details: Professional truck drivers seeking a better lifestyle on the road. Drivers who are currently paying for a home or apartment they rarely use and spending the majority of their time on the road. Drivers who feel underserved by current options — truck stops, gravel lots, side-of-road parking. Also: drivers interested in financial efficiency and lifestyle upgrades. Primary platform: Facebook and Instagram. Secondary: TikTok. National reach; Home Hub conversion tied to Memphis as first available location.

campaign_goal.primary_goal: Establish OneHome as the first and only nationwide home network built exclusively for professional truck drivers. Drive driver awareness, emotional connection, and membership signups — positioning OneHome as the obvious alternative to traditional housing for drivers who live life on the road.

key_messages: OneHome is the first nationwide home network designed specifically for professional truck drivers — resort-quality living, everywhere the road takes you.
Stop paying for a home you rarely use. OneHome lets you pay only for the days you need, and grow your membership as the network grows.
From truck stops and gravel lots to private club-level living — a mountain of value at a fraction of what you'd expect.

cta.text: Discover OneHome. Everywhere the road takes you.
cta.lead_capture_point: OneHome landing page — URL TBD (lander rebuild in progress)

budget.notes: Organic content first. Paid social TBD once assets are ready and lander is live. Brand package production, video production, and rendering enhancement costs TBD.

Full copy stored separately.

plan.strategic_context: OneHome is LineHaul Station's driver-facing lifestyle program — one of three primary sub-brands within the LHS ecosystem alongside FlexSpace (carrier/fleet program) and the Freight Relay program (TBD brand name). Where FlexSpace speaks to carriers and operators, OneHome speaks directly to the driver and their personal quality of life. The campaign's emotional premise is a genuine rags-to-riches lifestyle upgrade: from truck stops, gravel lots, and side-of-road parking to private, gated, resort-quality living that travels with the driver across the network. The financial premise is equally strong — drivers are paying for 365 days of a home they rarely use, while OneHome lets them pay only for days they actually need, grow incrementally, and access a fully refundable membership position. The campaign must communicate both the lifestyle aspiration and the financial intelligence simultaneously. The visual identity (deep red / carbon-fiber / paper-tear reveal) signals that this is something new and premium, distinctly separated from the main LHS palette. The tagline — Everywhere the Road Takes You — anchors the nationwide network concept and the emotional promise of home.

organic_plan_90_day.weekly_focus_notes: Phase 1 (Weeks 1–3): Brand package finalization, rendering completion, reel production. No publishing until assets are campaign-ready. Phase 2 (Weeks 4–6): Launch reveal reels and hook content. Lead with social scroll-stoppers before the brochure/explainer. Phase 3 (Weeks 7–12): Push Jeff Swinson explainer, financial savings messaging, and OneHome Lifestyle Calculator lander. Begin conversion push.

program_architecture.one_line_description: A private, resort-style home network built exclusively for professional truck drivers.
program_architecture.scroll_stop_hook: Imagine if the next time you searched for a home, there was a new type of listing — built just for truck drivers.
program_architecture.rags_to_riches_line: From truck stops and gravel lots to private club-level living — at a fraction of the cost you'd expect.
program_architecture.financial_hook: Stop paying for a home you rarely see. With OneHome, you pay only for the days you use.

home_hub_concept.definition: The Home Hub is the driver's primary location within the LineHaul Station network. It is their personal anchor point. NOTE: Do NOT use the phrase 'home base' — the military connotation is off-brand. The correct term is Home Hub.
what_makes_it_personal: Personal vehicle parking (covered or garage space)
home_hub_concept.what_it_is_not: The Home Hub is not the only place a driver feels at home. Every LHS location delivers the same experience. The Home Hub is simply where the personal items live.

network_experience.concept: Every LineHaul Station location delivers an identical high-quality experience. Same amenities. Same design. Same atmosphere. Same quality. Drivers feel at home at any location in the network, not just their Home Hub.
network_experience.experience_standard: Resort-style, private, gated, club-level living. Better than any home or apartment they've had. The lighting is right. The atmosphere is peaceful. The amenities are exceptional.
comparison_to_current_options: Apartment or condo they rarely use
Home they can't park their truck at

membership_model.concept: Drivers pay only for the days they plan to use within the network. They start with a small number of days and increase at any time, in any increment.
membership_model.flexibility: Start at 60 days/year. Add more whenever ready. Grow incrementally as the network expands and usage increases naturally.
membership_model.typical_trajectory: Many drivers will likely reach 200–250 days/year over time as the network grows nationwide.
membership_model.financial_advantage: No mortgage. No interest. No paying for 365 days when you only use 60. The membership fee is refundable (less transfer fee) — it builds like equity, not like rent.
membership_model.comparison: A $150,000 home requires $150,000 cash or a financed mortgage with ongoing interest. OneHome lets drivers build their membership incrementally, paying only for what they add, with no interest on unused days.
membership_model.savings_example: Drivers spending approximately $2,400/month on housing expenses could save over $1,200/month by switching to a OneHome 60-day plan.

amenities.name: Craft Kitchen
amenities.copy: A real kitchen for real meals. The Craft Kitchen gives you a clean, modern space to eat better and live better. Your days of eating whatever is under the heat lamp are over!

amenities.name: Sky Deck
amenities.copy: Kick back and take it all in. The Sky Deck is your open-air escape — perfect for relaxing, socializing, playing games, and enjoying a quiet space above the roar of the road.

amenities.name: Digital Den
amenities.copy: Unwind after a long day behind the wheel. An experiential center built for downtime, with games, entertainment, and a place to hang out with fellow drivers — or simply enjoy yourself on your own private screen.

amenities.name: Fitness Studio
amenities.copy: Stay strong on the road. The Fitness Studio gives you everything you need to move, stretch, and recharge so you feel your best mile after mile.

amenities.name: Laundry & More
amenities.copy: Clean clothes made easy. There's nothing better than feeling fresh and clean out on the road. Our modern laundry facilities give you everything you need to look your absolute best!

amenities.name: Gear Shop
amenities.copy: Everything a driver needs, right where you need it. It's all about convenience paired with quality and value. We're stocked with essentials, upgrades, and everyday items for life on the road.

sleeper_makeover.concept: In the OneHome lifestyle, the truck sleeper IS the bedroom. LineHaul Station can upgrade that experience through the Sleeper Makeover program.

lhs_programs.purpose: Nationwide lifestyle home network for drivers
Flexible terminal access program for freight operations
Operational efficiency through hub-based relay freight movement
lhs_programs.brand_name_status: TBD — candidates include RapidRelay, RelayExpress, FreightRelay, RelayOne

brand_within_lhs_ecosystem.lhs_one_liner: LineHaul Station is a full-service, member-only truck terminal network serving the industry through three programs: OneHome for drivers, FlexSpace for fleets, and [Relay Program] for freight movement.

visual_identity.signature_mechanic: Paper-tear reveal — carbon fiber dark red surface shakes/vibrates, then tears away with an authentic old-school paper rip to reveal rich amenity or lifestyle imagery underneath
visual_identity.palette_distinction: Deep red differentiates OneHome from primary LHS palette (gray-blue and orange). Any OneHome asset should read instantly as OneHome-branded.
visual_identity.typography: Industrial sans-serif mixed with warm premium display treatment. Strong hierarchy: LineHaul Station (primary), OneHome (sub-brand).
visual_identity.imagery_direction: Rich, lush, vibrant renderings and lifestyle photography. High contrast against the dark red base. The reveal should feel like a genuine upgrade being unwrapped.
visual_identity.tone: Upscale. Private. Resort-quality. Aspirational but real. Never cheesy. Never corporate. This is for drivers — it needs to feel earned and authentic.

campaign_summary_jeff_swinson: The LineHaul Station OneHome program is designed to give drivers a true sense of home while living life on the road. Rather than referring to a 'home base,' the concept centers around a Home Hub — the driver's primary location within the LineHaul Station network. At their Home Hub, drivers may have a mailbox, personal vehicle parking, and a storage unit. However, the broader concept of OneHome is that every LineHaul Station location delivers the same high-quality experience, so wherever a driver travels within the network, it still feels like home. As the network expands across the country, each location will offer the same amenities, design standards, and atmosphere — resort-style, private, and comfortable. In this lifestyle model, the sleeper cab of the truck becomes the driver's bedroom, and LineHaul Station can enhance that experience through the Sleeper Makeover program. The financial advantage is equally compelling: traditional home ownership requires paying for 365 days of housing whether the home is used or not. OneHome allows drivers to pay only for the days they actually plan to use, start small, and grow their membership incrementally as the network expands. Many drivers will likely reach 200–250 days per year over time. Each increase adds to a refundable membership position — no interest, no mortgage, no unused days. The goal of OneHome is to provide drivers with a nationwide home network that is flexible, financially efficient, and dramatically more comfortable than any option currently available on the road.

notes: OneHome is a sub-brand within the LHS ecosystem — always present as LineHaul Station / OneHome, never standalone. LHS is always the headline.
NEVER use 'home base' — the military inference is off-brand. Always 'Home Hub'.
The visual signature is the carbon-fiber tear-reveal. This mechanic should be treated as the OneHome brand moment the same way the scroll-stop hook works for Boots Walker.

The rags-to-riches framing is intentional and powerful — from gravel lots and truck stops to private club-level living — but it must always be paired with 'at a fraction of the cost you'd expect' to prevent sticker shock.
The Lifestyle Calculator is the most important conversion tool in this campaign. It makes the financial case personal and tangible. Do not launch the conversion push without it.
The brochure is a post-awareness asset — deliver it after the social hook or outreach call has already introduced the concept, not as a cold first impression.
OneHome may eventually justify its own Base44 build environment — flag for future development planning.

The scroll-stop social hook is: 'Imagine if the next time you searched for a home, there was a new type of listing — built just for truck drivers.' Use this as the primary opener for paid and organic content.
Boots Walker cameo at OneHome pool or amenity area would connect both campaigns and reinforce that LHS is a unified brand universe.
